etymology.description: Huái(淮) (huái, "Huai River") refers to the Huai River, an important river in China that serves as a geographical marker. Hǎi(海) (hǎi, "sea") refers to the sea, but in this context, it is part of a proper name rather than a reference to the ocean. Together, Huáihǎi(淮海) refers to a specific area that historically takes its name from the two elements, focusing on regions influenced by both the Huai River and the eastern seaboard. Dì(地) (dì, "earth; land") refers to land or ground, while qū(區) (qū, "region") denotes a specific district or area. Combining dì(地) and qū(區) yields the meaning "region".
etymology.interpretation: Huái refers to the Huai River, a distinct geographical feature, marking the northern part of the region. Hǎi, meaning "sea", traditionally indicated influences from the eastern seaboard or the connectivity to broader regions. The Huaihai combination denotes the historical and geographical significance of this area, associated with both river and coastal influences. Dìqū refers to the concept of a region or area, marking the specific territorial focus.

examples.chinese: 淮海地区(淮海地區)有丰富的历史文化。
examples.pinyin: Huáihǎi dìqū yǒu fēngfù de lìshǐ wénhuà.
examples.english: The Huaihai region has a rich historical culture.

examples.chinese: 淮海地区的美食让人难以忘怀(淮海地區的美食讓人難以忘懷)。
examples.pinyin: Huáihǎi dìqū de měishí ràng rén nányǐ wànghuái.
examples.english: The cuisine of the Huaihai region is unforgettable.

examples.chinese: 很多游客会来到淮海地区(很多遊客會來到淮海地區)，欣赏这里的自然风光(欣賞這裡的自然風光)。
examples.pinyin: Hěn duō yóukè huì láidào Huáihǎi dìqū, xīnshǎng zhèlǐ de zìrán fēngguāng.
examples.english: Many tourists come to the Huaihai region to enjoy the natural scenery here.
